g9.headline: G9 Capsule — Visible-Lamp Chandeliers & Decorative Sconces
g9.subheadline: When the bulb IS the design element, spec matters. CRI, dimming range, and compact form factor define the finished look.

roiCallout.headline: The Math on Callbacks
bullets: Average callback labor cost: $85–$150 per visit
Dimming complaints are the #1 LED callback category
One avoided callback pays for the premium on 10+ bulbs
Fewer color complaints = fewer credits issued to homeowners

mr16.headline: MR16 — Landscape, Track & Directional Accent
mr16.subheadline: Beam control, CRI, and outdoor durability separate professional-grade MR16 from commodity replacements.

professionalSpec.note: Built for buried and wet fixtures

roiCallout.headline: The Math on Callbacks
bullets: Landscape callback: $150+ per truck roll (time, gas, crew)
Replacing 20 commodity MR16s annually vs. zero with pro-spec
Beam spread mismatch = re-aim or re-lamp the entire run
One landscape re-lamp visit covers the premium on 30+ bulbs

g4.headline: G4 Bi-Pin — Low-Voltage Pendants & Under-Cabinet
g4.subheadline: Compact form factor and AC/DC compatibility are critical in low-voltage fixtures. The wrong G4 means flicker, buzz, or no-start.

roiCallout.headline: The Math on Callbacks
bullets: G4 flicker/buzz is the hardest LED complaint to diagnose remotely
Average troubleshooting visit: $120+ before the bulb is even replaced
AC/DC mismatch causes 40%+ of low-voltage LED failures
Universal compatibility eliminates the #1 diagnostic headache

e12.headline: E12 Candelabra — Decorative Chandeliers & Wall Sconces
e12.subheadline: The most visible bulb in residential lighting. CRI, dimming warmth, and filament aesthetics make or break the design.

industryStandard.note: Turns off before reaching mood level

professionalSpec.note: Install in high ceilings with confidence

roiCallout.headline: The Math on Callbacks
bullets: Chandelier re-lamp: $200+ with scaffolding for foyer installs
"It doesn't dim right" is the most common post-install complaint
Color mismatch between bulbs visible in multi-arm fixtures
Warm dim eliminates the "too blue when dimmed" objection entirely

gy6-35.headline: GY6.35 Bi-Pin — Low-Voltage Pendants & Specialty Fixtures
gy6-35.subheadline: The higher-wattage cousin of G4. Same AC/DC challenges, higher stakes — these run hotter and fail harder when spec'd wrong.

roiCallout.headline: The Math on Callbacks
bullets: GY6.35 failures often take out the transformer too — $300+ repair
Buzz complaints from low-voltage dimming are nearly impossible to solve remotely
Universal AC/DC compatibility eliminates transformer matching guesswork
Proper thermal management prevents cascade failures in multi-lamp fixtures

e26.headline: E26 Medium Base — The Commodity Uplift Opportunity
e26.subheadline: Everyone stocks E26. The question is whether your E26 makes the install look like a million bucks or a builder-grade flip.

industryStandard.note: Skin looks grey, wood looks flat

roiCallout.headline: The Math on Callbacks
bullets: E26 is the highest-volume base — small per-bulb premium, massive quality uplift
Color inconsistency across a room is the subtlest but most damaging complaint
3-step MacAdam binning ensures every bulb matches — zero re-sorting
Premium E26 in a builder fixture makes a $50 light look like $200
